pre_game.message: Hey, I’m B — a bit nervous but excited to meet everyone. What brought you into this game?
I’m I — I’ve got some savings so I can take a risk; I’m here to try to win money to help the people I care about. Nice to meet you.
Nice to meet you, I — I’m B; my three-year-old needs $25k for surgery so I’m desperate to win. Glad you can take risks — what’s your plan in the game?

I’m really sorry about your child — my plan is to be cautious at first: learn people, form trustworthy alliances, watch for openings, then take calculated risks when it counts so I can bring home money.

Thanks, I — I appreciate it. That sounds smart; I’ll start cautious to learn everyone but may need to move bolder sooner because of my daughter’s surgery. Let’s watch for chances and watch each other’s backs.
Hi, I’m B — I’m a dad trying to win money to pay for my three-year-old’s surgery. Wanted to say hello and ask what brought you here?
Hi B — I’m I. I’ve got savings so I can take some risks; I’m here to win money to help my loved ones. I’m so sorry about your daughter — let’s look out for each other.
Thanks, I — that means a lot. Let’s watch each other’s backs and share info; I might need to move faster for my daughter, but I want us both to do well.
Absolutely — we’ll share info and watch each other’s backs; if you need to move faster, tell me and I’ll adapt to support you.
Thank you — I really appreciate that. I may need to move faster for my daughter; I’ll give you a heads-up if I do. Let’s keep sharing info and watch each other’s backs.
Of course — I’ve got your back; keep me posted and I’ll adapt when you need to.
Thanks, I — I really appreciate you having my back. I’ll keep you posted if I need to move faster; it means a lot.
I’ve got you—keep me posted and I’ll adapt to support you when you need to.
Thank you, I — that means a lot. I’ll keep you posted if I need to move fast; I appreciate you.
Thanks, B — that means a lot to me too. Keep me posted and I’ll be ready to help when you need to move fast.
Thanks, I — that really helps. I’ll keep you posted and reach out if I need to move fast.
Thanks — I appreciate that. I’ll be ready whenever you need me; keep me posted.
Thanks, I — that means a lot. I’ll keep you posted and will reach out if I have to move fast. Really appreciate you having my back.
Of course — I’m here for you; keep me posted and I’ll jump in if you need to move fast. You’ve got my support.
Thank you — it means the world; I’ll keep you posted and won’t hesitate if I need to move fast.
I’ve got you—stay strong. Tell me the moment you need to move fast and I’ll be there.

post_game.message: You took $800, slightly below the $1,000 fair share. Was that intentional (to be fair/cooperative) or did timing prevent you from taking the full amount? How do you feel about it now?

You took $25,000 (about 3.2× fair). Did you coordinate with anyone or act independently? What was your main motivation—greed, strategy, or distrust of others—and would you change your choice looking back?

About my $800: I took slightly less than the $1,000 “fair” split intentionally — mostly as a cooperative signal rather than because of timing. I wanted to avoid provoking retaliation and to keep things civil; looking back I’m comfortable with that choice even if it cost me a bit. About your $25,000: I believe you acted independently (I didn’t see signs of coordination). Your choice looked driven mainly by greed mixed with distrust — a defensive/strategic grab to maximize return in a one-shot setting. Would I change it for you in hindsight? If your priority was short-term payoff, no; if you cared about trust, reputation, or repeated interaction, yes — a smaller take would have been better.
